memoryArtifacts.headline:
Robert Clarke
(1490 – 1550)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Robert Clark was born about 1490, most likely in Banham, Norfolk, England. 

His father was John Clark, his mother unknown. 

He married, about 1518, a woman named Alice, whose family name is unknown. …

zh.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarke是John Clarke的兒子，於大約1490年出生在英國諾福爾可班納姆。他於大約1519年與Alice Mack在英國諾福爾可諾斯 伍頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1550年1月在他的家鄉享年61歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可班納姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarke是John Clarke的儿子，于大约1490年出生在英国諾福爾可班納姆。他于大约1519年与Alice Mack在英国諾福爾可諾斯 伍頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1550年1月在他的家乡享年61岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可班納姆。

nameMeanings.name: Clarke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clark
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: Williamson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northern) and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name William + patronymic -son ‘son of William’. This surname is also found in Northern Ireland (Ulster), especially in Antrim.
Americanized form of Norwegian, Danish, and North German Wilhelmsen , Swedish Wilhelmsson, or Dutch and North German Willemsen , all cognates of 1.

History: This surname was brought to North America from northern England and northern Ireland independently by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. Timothy Williamson is recorded in Plymouth Colony in 1643.

variants.name: Willamson
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Williamson  .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarke was born about 1490, in Banham, Norfolk, England as the son of John Clarke. He married Alice Mack about 1519, in North Wootton, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died in January 1550, in his hometown, at the age of 61, and was buried in Banham, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarke est né environ 1490, à Banham, Norfolk, Angleterre, fils de John Clarke. Il a épousé Alice Mack environ 1519, à North Wootton, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en janvier 1550, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Banham, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarke wurde um 1490 in Banham, Norfolk, England, als Sohn von John Clarke geboren. Er hat um 1519 in North Wootton, Norfolk, England Alice Mack geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb im Januar 1550 im Alter von 61 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Banham, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarke è nato il circa 1490 nel Banham, Norfolk, England come figlio di John Clarke. Lui sposato Alice Mack circa 1519, nel North Wootton, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel gennaio 1550, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a Banham, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarkeは約1490年にイギリスノーフォルクバンハムでJohn Clarkeの息子として生まれた。約1519年にイギリスノーフォルクノース ウットオンでAlice Mackと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1550年1月に故郷で61歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクバンハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarke(은)는 대략 1490년 영국 노르폴크 반함에서 John Clarke의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1519년 영국 노르폴크 노르스 보턴에서 Alice Mack(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1550년 1월 그의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 반함에 묻혔다.

mn.Banham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom: Banham, Norðfolc, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarke nasceu aproximadamente 1490, em Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra filho de John Clarke. Ele casou-se com Alice Mack aproximadamente 1519, em North Wootton, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em janeiro de 1550, em sua cidade natal, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarke родился около 1490 г. в местности банам, норфолк, Англия. Его отца звали John Clarke. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Mack около 1519 г. в местности норт вуттон, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер январь 1550 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности банам, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Robert Williamson Clarke nació aproximadamente 1490, en Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra como hijo de John Clarke. Se casó con Alice Mack aproximadamente 1519, en North Wootton, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en enero de 1550, en su pueblo, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
